title: teline candicans var. umbellata
intro: Teline candicans var. umbellata, also known as Buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and North Africa, characterized by its thorny branches and small, yellow-green flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits of Teline candicans var. umbellata include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, Teline candicans var. umbellata is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from digestive issues to skin conditions.
